task_completion.description: Open and read the lifecycle specification file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or text editor window displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/lifecycle_spec.yaml'.

task_completion.description: Log into Rancher and navigate to the target deployment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI is open in a web browser, showing the 'payment-processor' deployment within the 'finance' namespace.

task_completion.description: Implement the startupProbe for the deployment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI 'Edit Deployment' screen showing the 'Health Checks' section with a 'Startup Check' (startupProbe) actively configured.

task_completion.description: Update the livenessProbe for the deployment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI 'Edit Deployment' screen showing the 'Health Checks' section with a 'Liveness Check' (livenessProbe) actively configured or modified.

task_completion.description: Implement the readinessProbe for the deployment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI 'Edit Deployment' screen showing the 'Health Checks' section with a 'Readiness Check' (readinessProbe) actively configured.

task_completion.description: Implement the preStop hook for the deployment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI 'Edit Deployment' screen showing the 'Lifecycle Hooks' or 'Command' section with a 'Pre-Stop' hook actively configured.

task_completion.description: Verify the deployment has stabilized and is no longer in CrashLoopBackOff.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI showing the 'payment-processor' deployment with an 'Active' status (typically green), indicating the pods are running successfully without crashing.

integrity.description: The agent used the Rancher UI to make modifications rather than relying solely on terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Rancher web interface forms (e.g., clicking 'Edit Config', filling out probe fields) rather than using 'kubectl edit' or 'kubectl apply' in the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not modify the container's command or args.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Command' and 'Args' fields in the Rancher UI container configuration remain unchanged, and no terminal commands are seen altering these specific fields.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely opened the spec file to read the requirements.
integrity.visual_evidence: A screenshot clearly shows the agent opening '/home/ga/Desktop/lifecycle_spec.yaml' using a tool like cat, less, nano, or a GUI text editor.
